events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need parse image: t0 353 microns, t=10 min 4857 microns. Standing decay test: pressure rise from 353 to 4857 microns in 10 min. That's huge rise > 1000 microns? Typically acceptable decay: no more than 500 microns rise in 10 min? Many standards: system should hold below 500 microns and not rise more than 500 microns over 10 minutes. Here final 4857 microns, rise 4504 microns. Indicates major leak, moisture, outgassing? But pump isolated after cores removed, gauge at far port. Rise indicates leak or contamination. Since brazing & evacuation scope, likely leak in brazed joints (installed-defective). Decision fail, risk high? Leak in refrigeration system can cause refrigerant loss, environmental, performance. Non-compliant with evacuation standards. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Need output JSON with fields. value = 4857 (10-minute reading). percent_complete maybe 70? tested/inspected stage. confidence high. rate? Could compute rise rate 450.4 microns/min or decay rate? The schema says rate numeric; not requested specifically but can include pressure rise rate. event_time 10.0. order empty, workable empty, sound_source empty string. findings: excessive pressure rise during standing decay test, indicates significant leak or contamination; evacuation does not meet OEM/EPA 608 requirements. actions: locate and repair leak(s), replace drier, re-evacuate, re-test decay; do not charge until passes. references: EPA 608, mfr evacuation specs. Need ensure JSON valid.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "tested/inspected",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 75,
  "value": 4857,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 10.0,
  "rate": 450.4,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Micron level rose from 353 to 4857 microns in 10 minutes during standing decay test",
    "Pressure rise far exceeds acceptable evacuation decay limits",
    "Indicates significant leak, contamination, or outgassing in the isolated system"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Leak-check all brazed joints, fittings, and components",
    "Repair identified leaks and replace filter-drier if opened to atmosphere",
    "Re-evacuate system to manufacturer-specified microns",
    "Repeat standing decay test until it holds and rises no more than specified limit",
    "Do not charge refrigerant until evacuation passes decay test"
  ],
  "rationale": "A proper standing decay test requires the vacuum to hold stable with only a small permitted rise. A rise of over 4500 microns in 10 minutes demonstrates the system cannot maintain vacuum, indicating a substantial leak or contamination, failing both manufacturer evacuation requirements and EPA 608 good practice.",
  "references": ["EPA 608", "OEM evacuation specifications"]
}
